metadata.note: Analytical benchmark -- scored by expert review. Claude CLI not available for live subagent runs.

expectations.text: Response provides the correct Sefaria endpoint URL with URL-encoded reference
expectations.evidence: Skill documents the exact Sefaria endpoint with URL encoding pattern

expectations.text: Response explains the text normalization steps (strip HTML, flatten array)
expectations.evidence: Skill includes the normalization pipeline: strip HTML, flatten array, trim

expectations.text: Response shows how to extract text from the json.text field
expectations.evidence: Skill documents the Sefaria response shape including the text field

expectations.text: Response mentions the Sefaria attribution requirement (CC BY-SA 2.0)
expectations.evidence: Skill specifies CC BY-SA 2.0 as the Sefaria attribution requirement

expectations.text: Response provides the correct Sefaria endpoint URL with URL-encoded reference
expectations.evidence: Sefaria API is publicly documented; agent would likely know the base endpoint

expectations.text: Response explains the text normalization steps (strip HTML, flatten array)
expectations.evidence: The html stripping and array flattening requirement is not obvious without testing the API response

expectations.text: Response shows how to extract text from the json.text field
expectations.evidence: json.text is the obvious field; agent would likely get this right

expectations.text: Response mentions the Sefaria attribution requirement (CC BY-SA 2.0)
expectations.evidence: Agent may omit attribution entirely or produce a different license string

notes: HTML normalization is the key trap -- Sefaria returns HTML-wrapped text that breaks plain display without stripping

expectations.text: Response provides the correct bible-api.com endpoint with translation=web parameter
expectations.evidence: Skill documents the exact bible-api.com endpoint and parameter format

expectations.text: Response identifies WEB as including deuterocanonicals
expectations.evidence: Skill notes WEB includes deuterocanonicals in the translation matrix

expectations.text: Response notes WEB is best for Catholic and Orthodox denominations
expectations.evidence: Skill recommends WEB for Catholic/Orthodox use due to deuterocanonical coverage

expectations.text: Response shows json.text extraction with whitespace normalization
expectations.evidence: Skill documents the response normalization including whitespace trim

expectations.text: Response provides the correct bible-api.com endpoint with translation=web parameter
expectations.evidence: bible-api.com is publicly known; agent would likely know the endpoint

expectations.text: Response identifies WEB as including deuterocanonicals
expectations.evidence: Deuterocanonical coverage of WEB is a detail not commonly known; agent may omit or get wrong

expectations.text: Response notes WEB is best for Catholic and Orthodox denominations
expectations.evidence: Denomination-translation mapping is ARE-specific; agent would not make this recommendation correctly

expectations.text: Response shows json.text extraction with whitespace normalization
expectations.evidence: Agent may omit the \n normalization step, producing raw formatted text

expectations.text: Response correctly identifies D&C 76:22 as a non-Bible LDS reference
expectations.evidence: Skill documents the isLdsBibleRef routing decision with D&C as non-Bible

expectations.text: Response explains isLdsBibleRef returns false for this reference
expectations.evidence: isLdsBibleRef and its logic are fully documented in the skill

expectations.text: Response identifies scriptures.nephi.org as the routing target
expectations.evidence: Skill explicitly names scriptures.nephi.org as the fetchNephiPassage endpoint

expectations.text: Response mentions LdsApiUnavailableError and the static fallback message
expectations.evidence: LdsApiUnavailableError and the churchofjesuschrist.org fallback message are documented

expectations.text: Response correctly identifies D&C 76:22 as a non-Bible LDS reference
expectations.evidence: Without the routing guide, agent may attempt to look up D&C 76:22 on bible-api.com which would 404

expectations.text: Response explains isLdsBibleRef returns false for this reference
expectations.evidence: isLdsBibleRef is ARE-specific; agent would not know it exists

expectations.text: Response identifies scriptures.nephi.org as the routing target
expectations.evidence: scriptures.nephi.org is obscure; agent would likely suggest a different non-existent or incorrect API

expectations.text: Response mentions LdsApiUnavailableError and the static fallback message
expectations.evidence: ARE-specific error type and fallback not derivable without skill

notes: D&C routing is 100% ARE-specific -- without the skill, agent would route to bible-api.com and get a 404
Eval 3 (D&C routing) scores 0/4 without skill -- agent would route D&C 76:22 to bible-api.com and get a 404
Eval 1 partial credit (0.5) without skill -- agent knows Sefaria API but misses HTML normalization and attribution
Routing decision tree added in v1.1.0 is the highest-impact content addition -- replaces a multi-paragraph read with a scannable tree
HTML stripping on Sefaria responses is the most common trap -- Sefaria wraps text in <i> tags and array-of-strings format
Analytical benchmark: scored by expert review. Run live evals with: node .agents/skills/skill-creator/scripts/run_eval.py (requires claude CLI)
